channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Author Correction: Magnon confinement in epitaxial antiferromagnetic oxide heterostructures
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 23 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02612-8

Author Correction: Magnon confinement in epitaxial antiferromagnetic oxide heterostructures
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Magnon confinement in epitaxial antiferromagnetic oxide heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Sajid Husain

Manuel Bibes

Ramamoorthy Ramesh

item.title: To bioprint morphogenic organoids it is essential to chill out and relax
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 23 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02581-y

High-throughput and controlled organoid fabrication is achieved by combining 3D bioprinting, matrix biochemistry and stress relaxation in packed hydrogel particles.
item.dc:title: To bioprint morphogenic organoids it is essential to chill out and relax
item.dc:creator: Christopher B. Highley
Thomas E. Angelini

item.title: Sub-wavelength extreme ultraviolet microscopy reveals domain-wall stability during ultrafast demagnetization
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 20 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02583-w

Femtosecond extreme ultraviolet microscopy is used to directly image domain-wall dynamics during laser-induced demagnetization. The domain walls are found to be stable up to the onset of irreversible magnetic switching.
item.dc:title: Sub-wavelength extreme ultraviolet microscopy reveals domain-wall stability during ultrafast demagnetization
item.dc:creator: Hung-Tzu Chang
Sergey Zayko
Timo Schmidt
Ofer Kfir
Murat Sivis
Johan H. Mentink
Manfred Albrecht
Claus Ropers

item.title: The data-only illusion in materials discovery
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 17 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02578-7

Artificial intelligence may have transformed image and language generation, but in materials science, data scarcity and synthesis complexity demand a different approach. Only by coupling artificial intelligence with deep chemical insight can we turn virtual predictions into real materials.
item.dc:title: The data-only illusion in materials discovery
item.dc:creator: Berend Smit
Susana Garcia

item.title: Contact-reaction-triggered selective doping
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 16 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02582-x

A gold-surface-mediated doping strategy offers a solution to achieve spatially resolved chemical doping in organic semiconductors, enabling facile control for high-performance organic devices.
item.dc:title: Contact-reaction-triggered selective doping
item.dc:creator: Dongyang Wang
Chong-an Di

item.title: Two-dimensional hard masks push three-dimensional patterning
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 16 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02571-0

Ultrathin van der Waals metal oxyhalides act as robust hard masks for three-dimensional semiconductor processing. Their exceptional plasma resistance and unique plasma-induced smoothing enable high-aspect-ratio etching and precise nanopatterning of various materials.
item.dc:title: Two-dimensional hard masks push three-dimensional patterning
item.dc:creator: Jihoon Chang
Chul-Ho Lee

item.title: A nitride-based non-volatile memory enabled by electric-field-induced phase transition
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 16 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02565-y

A non-volatile Al0.7Sc0.3N-based memory is developed with low switching voltage, fast write speed, low energy consumption and ~108-cycle write endurance at 583 K, with excellent stability and uniformity.
item.dc:title: A nitride-based non-volatile memory enabled by electric-field-induced phase transition
item.dc:creator: Tao Zeng
Zhongran Liu
Youdi Gu
Bingjie Dang
Yuan Gao
Tianlong Xu
Peng Li
Shu Shi
Kaixuan Sun
Yao Zhu
Xiao Gong
He Tian
Jingsheng Chen

item.title: Observation of strong tripartite coupling in a cavity-quantum circuit-antiferromagnet platform
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 16 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02585-8

Hybridized modes are realized through strong tripartite coupling between a three-dimensional microwave cavity, a granular-aluminium superconducting thin-film microwave resonator circuit and an antiferromagnetic crystal, with implications for sensing and for frequency conversion between the microwave and terahertz regimes.
item.dc:title: Observation of strong tripartite coupling in a cavity-quantum circuit-antiferromagnet platform
item.dc:creator: C. Fruy
A. Théry
B. Hue
W. Legrand
L. Jarjat
J. Bally
J. Craquelin
M. R. Delbecq
A. Cottet
T. Kontos
